name: Orgi Forest Walk
description: Enjoy an easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) circular jogging route through the ancient Orgi Forest, featuring ponds and a birdwatching observatory.
region_intro: Jogging routes Ultzama traverse a landscape characterized by lush, green scenery, rolling meadows, and dense forests. The region features significant natural areas like the ancient Orgi Forest, an 80-hectare humid oak grove with well-maintained paths. Surrounding mountain ranges such as Urbasa-Andía and Aralar provide a varied backdrop for running. This diverse terrain offers a range of jogging experiences, from gentle forest paths to more challenging routes through hills and valleys. Best jogging routes around Ultzama The most popular jogging route is Orgi Forest Walk, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes about 29 minutes to complete. This easy route leads through an ancient oak forest with accessible, flat paths. Another top favourite among local runners is View over the valley loop from Atetz, a moderate 3.8 miles (6.2 km) path. This route offers scenic views across the Ultzama valley. Local runners also love the Beuntzako Gaina – Itxondoa loop from Atetz, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through meadows and charming villages, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Jogging around Ultzama is defined by lush meadows, dense oak forests, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy forest walks to more challenging mountain running. The routes in Ultzama are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 280 runners have used komoot to explore Ultzama's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Ultzama?
A: Ultzama offers a diverse network of nearly 20 running routes. These include 2 easy trails perfect for a relaxed jog, 6 moderate paths for a good workout, and 10 more challenging routes for experienced runners seeking a greater adventure.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Ultzama's running trails?
A: Ultzama's running trails traverse a picturesque landscape of lush, green scenery, rolling meadows, and dense forests. You'll encounter ancient oak groves, some with monumental trees, and enjoy the backdrop of surrounding mountain ranges like Urbasa-Andía and Aralar. The region is often called the 'Switzerland of Navarre' due to its varied natural beauty.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families in Ultzama?
A: Yes, Ultzama has several easy running routes ideal for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971557088/orgi-forest-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orgi Forest Walk&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering well-maintained, flat, and accessible paths through an ancient oak forest. It's a 4.1 km (2.5 miles) trail that takes about 29 minutes to complete, perfect for a gentle jog.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Ultzama?
A: Absolutely. Many of Ultzama's running paths are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24675646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View over the valley loop from Atetz&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic vistas, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15031546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beuntzako Gaina – Itxondoa loop from Atetz&lt;/a&gt; guides you through meadows and charming villages.

Q: What are some notable natural features or attractions to see while running in Ultzama?
A: While running in Ultzama, you can explore significant natural areas like the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971557088/orgi-forest-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orgi Forest&lt;/a&gt;, an ecologically rich humid oak grove. The region also boasts monumental oak and chestnut groves, some declared Natural Monuments. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3149161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eltxumendi Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Sayoa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the running trails?
A: Yes, Ultzama's varied landscape offers numerous scenic spots. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24675646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View over the valley loop from Atetz&lt;/a&gt; provide stunning vistas across the Ultzama valley. You can also find specific viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3373978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leateko Gaina Rock Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering memorable panoramas of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Ultzama?
A: The running routes in Ultzama are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's lush, green scenery, the tranquility of the ancient forests, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels. The well-maintained paths, especially in areas like Orgi Forest, are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Ultzama caters to experienced runners with several challenging routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6977048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Irurtzun Gorge – View of the Dos Hermanas Gorge loop from Imotz&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.5 km (7.8 miles) trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24645577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erga – Ahizpa Txikia loop from Imotz&lt;/a&gt;, spanning nearly 16 km (9.9 miles) with substantial climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Ultzama?
A: Ultzama is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly popular for running. In spring, the meadows are vibrant green, and wildflowers bloom. Autumn transforms the forests into a spectacle of colors, and it's also the season for mushroom foraging, especially around the Ultzama Mycological Park. Summers are generally pleasant, while winters can offer a unique, crisp running experience.

Q: Are there places to park near the running trails in Ultzama?
A: While specific parking details vary by route, many trailheads, especially at popular recreational areas like the Orgi Forest, offer designated parking facilities. It's advisable to check the individual route descriptions on komoot for the most accurate information on parking availability for your chosen running path.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or restaurants near the running routes?
A: Ultzama is dotted with charming villages that offer local hospitality. While not directly on every trail, you'll often find cafes and restaurants in nearby villages where you can refuel after your run. These establishments provide a taste of Basque rural traditions and local cuisine.
tips:
The Orgi Forest is an oak forest located in the picturesque Ultzama Valley, just 30 minutes from Pamplona. It is about 80 hectares of ancient forest of humid oak groves of Navarra. with great ecological interest.

El Bosque has various trails of very low difficulty, flat and accessible, ideal for a quick and quiet return.

faqs.question: How long does it take to run the Bosque de Orgi circular route?
faqs.answer: The main circular route, which is approximately 4.1 km long, typically takes around 29 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. This duration is based on an average pace and can vary depending on your speed and any stops you make.

faqs.question: Is the Bosque de Orgi – running circular suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is excellent for beginners. It's classified as 'easy' with very little elevation change, making it accessible and gentle. The trails are generally flat and well-maintained, perfect for those new to trail jogging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running circular?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by its simplicity, flatness, and accessibility. You'll find smooth, well-cared-for paths, with many sections designed to be suitable for all fitness levels. While there might be a slightly narrower section near lagoons, the path remains easy to navigate. Comfortable, sporty footwear is recommended.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll jog through the ancient humid oak forest, a rare and ecologically significant site. The scenery includes diverse vegetation, undergrowth, and several ponds and lagoons. Keep an eye out for a birdwatching observatory and a unique 'labyrinth' trail. The forest itself, known as Orgi Forest, is the main highlight.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter while jogging?
faqs.answer: The forest is home to various fauna. You might spot amphibians like the marbled newt and the San Antonio frog, especially near the ponds and lagoons. Birdwatchers can utilize the observatory along the route to observe different bird species.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The forest's beauty is particularly striking in spring and autumn, offering a 'mosaic of sensations and colors.' These seasons provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for jogging.

faqs.question: Is the Bosque de Orgi – running circular dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Bosque de Orgi is generally dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the running circular?
faqs.answer: The Bosque de Orgi is a protected Natural Recreational Area, and there are designated parking areas available for visitors. Look for signs directing you to the main recreational area's parking facilities, which provide convenient access to the circular running route.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Orgi Forest?
faqs.answer: The Bosque de Orgi operates as a Natural Recreational Area. While specific permits for jogging are typically not required, it's always a good idea to check the official website or local information boards for any current regulations or potential entrance fees, especially for certain facilities or events within the park.

faqs.question: Is the trail accessible for all fitness levels or those with reduced mobility?
faqs.answer: Yes, a notable feature of the Bosque de Orgi is its inclusivity. Many sections of the trails are specifically designed to be accessible for all fitness levels, including people with reduced mobility or even in wheelchairs. This makes it a great option for a wide range of visitors.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the running circular route within the Bosque de Orgi utilizes sections of several local trails. You will find yourself on parts of Orgiko basoaren ibilaldia, Lizasobidea, Bentaldea, and Orgibidea, among others, as you complete the loop.
